title: Distinction between sovereign/governmental acts and acts of a private law nature

evidence.clean_text: 26.	International law recognises a distinction between acts done by a state in the exercise of sovereign or governmental authority (ie, acts done jure imperii ), and acts done by it of a private law nature (acts done jure gestionis), typically (but not exclusively) commercial activities: Benkharbouche , [8]. That distinction is important to the first issue I have to resolve.
